question_title: What will the Ifo Business Climate Index level (points) for Germany be for February 2026?
summary: The Ifo Business Climate Index for February 2026 is forecast to be close to the January 2026 reading of 87.6. The index has been flat at 87.6 for two consecutive months after declining from a peak of 89.0 in August 2025. Key factors: (1) Trump's suspension of Greenland-related tariffs on Jan 21 is modestly positive for sentiment; (2) German fiscal stimulus and ECB rate cuts provide gradual support; (3) However, 15% reciprocal EU tariff remains active and trade uncertainty persists; (4) Manufacturing still under strain and Bundesbank describes recovery as "slowly getting started." Monte Carlo simulation using Student-t distribution (df=12) with empirical monthly volatility of 0.67 points and slight positive drift of +0.1 produces a distribution centered around 87.7.

factors.description: Current level anchoring: Jan 2026 at 87.6, unchanged from Dec 2025. Strong anchor for next month given typical month-to-month changes of <1 point.
Recent downtrend from Aug peak (89.0 to 87.6). Last 4 months essentially flat/declining, suggesting sideways movement.
Trump suspended Greenland-related tariffs on Jan 21 - modestly positive for German business sentiment, but 15% reciprocal EU tariff remains.
German fiscal stimulus and ECB rate cuts expected to boost growth (Goldman forecasts 1.1% for 2026). Gradual improvement in outlook.
Ongoing trade policy uncertainty - tariffs could be reimposed. Manufacturing under strain. Bundesbank says recovery 'slowly getting started'.
Historical monthly volatility: std of ~0.67 points. Typical month-to-month change ranges from -1.3 to +1.4 in recent data.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Ifo Business Climate Index for February 2026**

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| 10th | 86.8 |
| 20th | 87.1 |
| 40th | 87.5 |
| 60th | 87.9 |
| 80th | 88.3 |
| 90th | 88.6 |

**Key findings:**
- The index has been flat at **87.6** for two consecutive months (Dec 2025, Jan 2026), down from its 2025 peak of 89.0 in August
- **Positive factors**: Trump's suspension of Greenland-related tariffs (Jan 21), German fiscal stimulus, ECB rate cuts
- **Negative factors**: 15% reciprocal EU tariff still active, trade policy uncertainty, manufacturing weakness
- **Monte Carlo simulation** using empirical monthly volatility (std=0.67) with Student-t distribution centers the forecast at ~87.7, essentially flat from January
- The 80% confidence interval spans roughly 86.8 to 88.6 points
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) What will the Ifo Business Climate Index level (points) for Germany be for February 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42038)
